weakref/ProxyType.name: ProxyType
weakref/ProxyType.description: The type object for proxies of objects which are not callable.

info.excerpt: The type object for proxies of objects which are not callable.

weakref/ProxyTypes.name: ProxyTypes
weakref/ProxyTypes.description: Sequence containing all the type objects for proxies. This can make it simpler to test if an object is a proxy without being dependent on naming both proxy types.

info.excerpt: Sequence containing all the type objects for proxies. This can make it simpler to test if an object is a proxy without being dependent on naming both proxy types.

weakref/proxy.name: proxy
weakref/proxy.description: Return a proxy to object which uses a weak reference. This supports use of the proxy in most contexts instead of requiring the explicit dereferencing used with weak reference objects. The returned object will have a type of either ProxyType or CallableProxyType, depending on whether object is callable. Proxy objects are not hashable regardless of the referent; this avoids a number of problems related to their fundamentally mutable nature, and prevents their use as dictionary keys. callback is...

weakref/finalize/peek.name: peek
weakref/finalize/peek.description: If self is alive then return the tuple (obj, func, args, kwargs). If self is dead then return None.

info.excerpt: If self is alive then return the tuple (obj, func, args, kwargs). If self is dead then return None.
